examples.jp: 1985年までに、以前の試みは全て失敗していた。

pron.tts: By 1985, previous attempts had all failed.

items.meaning: evidence/information/advice/research/progress は不可算。複数形・a/many は不可。much/a lot of を使う

examples.en: There is a lot of evidence for this.
examples.jp: これには多くの証拠がある。（×many evidences）

pron.tts: There is a lot of evidence for this.

items.front: move の用法（移住 vs 移動）
items.meaning: move は『永続的に住む場所を移す』時に使う。日常の移動には go を使う

examples.en: I go to work by train.
examples.jp: 電車で通勤します。（×I move to work by train）

pron.tts: I go to work by train.

passages.jp: 古代の人々がどのようにポリネシアの島々に最初に定住したのかは、長年の懸念（謎）だった。原始的な道具だけでアジアから東へ渡った、大胆な先駆者＝熟練の船乗りたちだと考える人もいる。資源が乏しい中、彼らは頑丈ないかだを組み立て、星で航海して自分の方角を知らねばならなかった。トール・ヘイエルダールはその真実を確かめようと、コンティキ号と名づけたいかだでペルーから探検を率いた。好都合な風と、食料の供給源となる魚のおかげで、長い航海が可能になった。

sentences.en: For years it was a real concern how the ancient people first came to settle the Polynesian islands.
sentences.linking: For years‿it was‿a real concern how the‿ancient people first came to settle the Polynesian islands.
tips: 強勢: YEARS, con·CERN, AN·cient, SET·tle, Poly·NE·sian

sentences.en: Some believe bold pioneers, skilled seafarers, sailed east using only primitive tools.
sentences.linking: Some believe bold pioneers, skilled seafarers, sailed‿east‿using‿only primitive tools.
tips: 強勢: be·LIEVE, pio·NEERS, SEA·farers, EAST, PRI·mitive

sentences.en: With few resources available, they had to construct sturdy rafts and navigate by the stars to find their bearings.
sentences.linking: With few resources‿available, they had to construct sturdy rafts‿and navigate by the stars to find‿their bearings.
tips: 強勢: re·SOURCES, a·VAIL·able, con·STRUCT, NA·vigate, BEAR·ings

sentences.en: Thor Heyerdahl set out to discover the truth, leading an expedition from Peru on a raft he dubbed Kon-Tiki.
sentences.linking: Thor Heyerdahl set‿out to discover the truth, leading‿an expedition from Peru on‿a raft he dubbed Kon-Tiki.
tips: 連結: set‿out=セッタウト, leading‿an, on‿a
強勢: dis·CO·ver, TRUTH, expe·DI·tion, Pe·RU, DUBBED

sentences.en: Favourable winds and fish as a source of food made the long voyage possible.
sentences.linking: Favourable winds‿and fish‿as‿a source‿of food made the long voyage possible.
tips: 強勢: FA·vourable, WINDS, SOURCE, VOY·age, POS·sible

passages.jp: プラスチックは、あらゆる種類の製品に形成できることから、おそらく最も便利な発明の一つだ。防水のコーティングはケーブルを守り、合成のボトルは軽くて安価だ。この発想は最近生じたものではなく、人類は中世から簡単なプラスチックを使ってきた。本当の転機は1907年に訪れたが、ある初期の発明家は成功前に倒産した。今日ではプラスチックの環境への影響が懸念の高まりとなり、生分解性ボトルが世に出された。

sentences.en: Plastic is arguably one of the most useful inventions, since it can be formed into a huge variety of products.
sentences.linking: Plastic‿is‿arguably one‿of the most useful‿inventions, since‿it can be formed‿into‿a huge variety‿of products.
tips: 強勢: AR·guably, USE·ful, FORMED, va·RI·ety, PRO·ducts

sentences.en: A waterproof coating protects cables, while synthetic bottles are light and inexpensive.
sentences.linking: A waterproof coating protects cables, while synthetic bottles‿are light‿and‿inexpensive.
tips: 強勢: WA·terproof, COAT·ing, syn·THE·tic, inex·PEN·sive

sentences.en: The idea did not originate recently; mankind has used simple plastics since the medieval period.
sentences.linking: The‿idea did not‿originate recently; mankind has‿used simple plastics since the medieval period.
tips: 強勢: i·DE·a, O·riginate, man·KIND, me·DI·eval

sentences.en: The real turning point came in 1907, though one early inventor went bankrupt before success.
sentences.linking: The real turning point came‿in 1907, though‿one early‿inventor went bankrupt before success.
tips: 強勢: REAL, TURN·ing POINT, in·VEN·tor, BANK·rupt, suc·CESS

sentences.en: Today the environmental impact of plastic is a growing concern, so biodegradable bottles have been launched.
sentences.linking: Today the‿environmental‿impact‿of plastic‿is‿a growing concern, so biodegradable bottles have‿been launched.
tips: 強勢: to·DAY, en·viron·MEN·tal, IM·pact, con·CERN, LAUNCHED

passages.jp: 1912年にタイタニックがアイルランドを出発した時、それが沈むと疑う者はほとんどいなかった。当初、乗組員は氷山警告を無視し、救命ボートの致命的な不足が悲劇を生んだ。何十年もの間、残骸を見つける以前の試みはすべて失敗した。やがてフランスのチームと協力して、探検家たちは海底で驚くべき発見をした——船体は70年の海中で激しく劣化していたが。

sentences.en: When the Titanic departed from Ireland in 1912, few had any doubt it was unsinkable.
sentences.linking: When the Titanic departed from Ireland in 1912, few had‿any doubt‿it was unsinkable.
tips: 連結: had‿any=ハダニ, doubt‿it=ダウティッ(tは弱く)
強勢: Ti·TA·nic, de·PAR·ted, DOUBT, un·SINK·able

sentences.en: Initially, the crew ignored the iceberg warnings, and a fatal lack of lifeboats turned the night into a tragedy.
sentences.linking: Initially, the crew ignored the iceberg warnings, and‿a fatal lack‿of lifeboats turned the night‿into‿a tragedy.
tips: 連結: lack‿of=ラッコヴ, night‿into=ナイティントゥ
強勢: i·NI·tially, ICE·berg, FA·tal, LACK, TRA·gedy

sentences.en: For decades, previous attempts to find the wreck all failed.
sentences.linking: For decades, previous‿attempts to find the wreck‿all failed.
tips: 連結: previous‿attempts, wreck‿all=レッコール
強勢: DE·cades, at·TEMPTS, WRECK, FAILED
注意音: wreckのwは無音(/r/), V(previous)

sentences.en: Then, in collaboration with a French team, explorers made an astonishing discovery on the seabed — though the hull had badly deteriorated after seventy years underwater.
sentences.linking: Then, in collaboration with‿a French team, explorers made‿an astonishing discovery‿on the seabed — though the hull had badly deteriorated after seventy years underwater.
tips: 連結: made‿an=メイダン, discovery‿on=ディスカヴァリオン
強勢: collabo·RA·tion, FRENCH, a·STON·ishing, dis·CO·very

passages.jp: アイダホでビーバーは大迷惑になり、そのダム建設が地形全体を変え始めた。作る本能は止まらず、人家が彼らの生息地を侵害し始めていた。そこで猟区管理人は食料が豊富な理想的な谷を見つけた。第二次大戦の余剰パラシュートを使い、飛行機からビーバーを落とすという実にユニークな解決策をとった。

sentences.en: Beavers became such a nuisance in Idaho that their dam construction began to alter the whole landscape.
sentences.linking: Beavers became such‿a nuisance‿in Idaho that their dam construction began to alter the whole landscape.
tips: 連結: such‿a=サッチャ, nuisance‿in
強勢: NUI·sance, I·da·ho, con·STRUC·tion, AL·ter
注意音: TH(that/their/the), nuisance=ニューサンス

sentences.en: Their instinct to build never stops, and homes had begun to encroach on their habitat.
sentences.linking: Their‿instinct to build never stops, and homes had begun to encroach‿on their habitat.
tips: 連結: encroach‿on=インクローチョン, homes‿had
強勢: IN·stinct, BUILD, be·GUN, en·CROACH, HA·bitat
注意音: TH(their), instinctの-nct連続子音

sentences.en: So wardens found an ideal valley with an abundance of food.
sentences.linking: So wardens found‿an‿ideal valley with‿an‿abundance‿of food.
tips: 連結: found‿an=ファウンダン, with‿an=ウィザン, abundance‿of
強勢: WAR·dens, i·DE·al, a·BUN·dance, FOOD

sentences.en: Using a surplus of war parachutes, they dropped the beavers from a plane — a truly unique solution.
sentences.linking: Using‿a surplus‿of war parachutes, they dropped the beavers from‿a plane — a truly‿unique solution.
tips: 強勢: SUR·plus, PA·rachutes, u·NIQUE, so·LU·tion
注意音: R(surplus/war), unique=ユニーク /juːˈniːk/

passages.jp: ダンスは他に類を見ないほど脳を刺激する。多少の不確かさはあるが、一説では、音楽と動きが組み合わさってドーパミンを生み出す点が重要な要因だという。脳はパターンを予測するのが好きで、予見できない複雑な動きを見ると強烈な快感が湧く——私たちがダンスフロアを愛する理由は明らかだ。

sentences.en: Dancing stimulates the brain like little else.
sentences.linking: Dancing stimulates the brain like‿little‿else.
tips: 連結: little‿else=リトゥルエルス(L連結)
強勢: DAN·cing, STI·mulates, BRAIN, LIT·tle
注意音: stim-の/st/クラスター, littleのt(米=フラップ/英=明瞭)

sentences.en: Despite some uncertainty, one theory is that a key factor is how music and movement combine to generate dopamine.
sentences.linking: Despite some‿uncertainty, one theory‿is that‿a key factor‿is how music‿and movement combine to generate dopamine.
tips: 連結: factor‿is=ファクタリズ, music‿and
強勢: uncer·TAIN·ty, THEO·ry, FAC·tor, GE·nerate, DO·pamine

sentences.en: The brain loves to predict patterns, so watching a complex move we cannot foresee creates an intense rush of pleasure — it is obvious why we love the dance floor.
sentences.linking: The brain loves to predict patterns, so watching‿a complex move we cannot foresee creates‿an‿intense rush‿of pleasure — it‿is‿obvious why we love the dance floor.
tips: 連結: watching‿a=ワッチンガ, rush‿of=ラッショヴ, it‿is=イティズ
強勢: pre·DICT, PAT·terns, com·PLEX, in·TENSE, OB·vious

passages.jp: AI芸術は創造性に対する私たちの見方を揺さぶる。当初、機械が絵を描けるという考え方は懐疑をもって受け止められたが、その結果は専門家を騙すほど本物らしかった。この革新的技術は価値について難しい問いを投げかける。原画の希少性がその価値を生むなら、複製はどうなるのか。専門家と一般人がこうした芸術を解釈する仕方には大きな隔たりがあり、自動化が人間の芸術家に取って代わるという恐れもある。

sentences.en: AI art challenges our perspective on creativity.
sentences.linking: AI‿art challenges‿our perspective‿on creativity.
tips: 強勢: AI ART, CHAL·lenges, per·SPEC·tive, crea·TI·vity
注意音: R(art/our/perspective), creativity=クリエイ·TI·ヴィティ

sentences.en: At first, the notion that machines could paint was met with scepticism, yet the results were credible enough to fool experts.
sentences.linking: At first, the notion that machines could paint was met‿with scepticism, yet the results were credible‿enough to fool experts.
tips: 連結: met‿with=メットウィズ, credible‿enough
強勢: NO·tion, ma·CHINES, SCEP·ticism, CRE·dible, e·NOUGH
注意音: TH(the/that/with), scepticism=スケプティスィズム

sentences.en: This innovative technology raises hard questions about value: the scarcity of an original gives it worth, so where does that leave a copy?
sentences.linking: This‿innovative technology raises hard questions‿about value: the scarcity‿of‿an‿original gives‿it worth, so where does that leave‿a copy?
tips: 連結: of‿an, gives‿it=ギヴズィッ, scarcity‿of
強勢: IN·novative, tech·NO·logy, SCAR·city, o·RI·ginal

sentences.en: There is a real disparity between how experts and the public interpret such art — and a fear that automation will replace human artists.
sentences.linking: There‿is‿a real disparity between how experts‿and the public‿interpret such‿art — and‿a fear that‿automation will replace human‿artists.
tips: 連結: there‿is‿a=ゼアリザ, experts‿and, such‿art
強勢: dis·PA·rity, ex·PERTS, in·TER·pret, auto·MA·tion, re·PLACE

passages.themeName: 才能ある子どもの学校
passages.jp: 当校は才能ある子どもの能力を育むために存在する。明確な構想に従い、生徒を甘やかすのではなく、自立と自主性を育てる。自分で物事を行うことは家族と離れている時間を埋め合わせ、創造性を刺激し、本当の成長を促す。

sentences.en: Our school exists to foster the talents of gifted children.
sentences.linking: Our school‿exists to foster the talents‿of gifted children.
tips: 連結: talents‿of=タレンツォヴ, school‿exists
強勢: ex·ISTS, FOS·ter, TA·lents, GIF·ted, CHIL·dren

sentences.en: We follow a clear blueprint: rather than coddle pupils, we build their self-reliance and independence.
sentences.linking: We follow‿a clear blueprint: rather than coddle pupils, we build their self-reliance‿and‿independence.
tips: 強勢: FOL·low, BLUE·print, RA·ther, COD·dle, inde·PEN·dence

sentences.en: Doing things alone compensates for time away from family, sparks creativity, and drives real progress.
sentences.linking: Doing things‿alone compensates for time‿away from family, sparks creativity, and drives real progress.
tips: 連結: time‿away, away‿from=アウェイフロム, things‿alone
強勢: COM·pensates, FA·mily, crea·TI·vity, PRO·gress

passages.jp: オンラインで一夜にして成功した有名な起業家が、二つの大都市圏を結ぶ画期的な列車に投資することを決めた。だが障害があった。運賃は一般の人が払えないほど法外かもしれない。会社が地元政府を説得して計画を認可させるまで、未来の列車は設計図のままだ。

sentences.en: A famous entrepreneur who achieved overnight success online decided to invest in a groundbreaking train linking two metropolitan areas.
sentences.linking: A famous‿entrepreneur who‿achieved‿overnight success‿online decided to‿invest‿in‿a groundbreaking train linking two metropolitan‿areas.
tips: 連結: invest‿in=インヴェスティン, achieved‿overnight, in‿a
強勢: FA·mous, entrepre·NEUR, suc·CESS, in·VEST, GROUND·breaking
注意音: R(entrepreneur/areas/train), entrepreneur=アントレプレ·ヌァ

sentences.en: But there were obstacles. Tickets might be too exorbitant for ordinary people to afford.
sentences.linking: But there‿were‿obstacles. Tickets might be too‿exorbitant for‿ordinary people to‿afford.
tips: 強勢: OB·stacles, TI·ckets, ex·OR·bitant, OR·dinary, af·FORD

sentences.en: Until the company can persuade local governments to authorise the project, the train of the future stays on the drawing board.
sentences.linking: Until the company can persuade local governments to‿authorise the project, the train‿of the future stays‿on the drawing board.
tips: 連結: train‿of, stays‿on=ステイゾン
強勢: COM·pany, per·SUADE, AU·thorise, PRO·ject, DRAW·ing BOARD

passages.themeName: マリア・モンテッソーリ
passages.jp: マリア・モンテッソーリは、すべての子どもの学ぶ権利のために奮闘した。名家に生まれ、医学部で唯一の女性として耐え抜き、優秀な成績で卒業した。貧しい子どもたちと働く中で、すべての子は生まれつき学びたい願望を持つという理論を思いついた。彼女の手法は自由と責任を強調し、すぐに世界中で採用された。生涯平和の擁護者であり、その後の教育への影響は今も続いている。

sentences.en: Maria Montessori struggled for every child's right to learn.
sentences.linking: Maria Montessori struggled for‿every child's‿right to learn.
tips: 強勢: Ma·RI·a, Montes·SO·ri, STRUG·gled, RIGHT, LEARN

sentences.en: From an esteemed family, she persevered as the only woman in medical school and graduated with top grades.
sentences.linking: From‿an‿esteemed family, she persevered‿as the‿only woman‿in medical school‿and graduated with top grades.
tips: 連結: from‿an=フロマン, persevered‿as, woman‿in
強勢: es·TEEMED, perse·VERED, ON·ly, ME·dical, GRA·duated

sentences.en: Working with poor children, she came up with the theory that all kids have an innate desire to learn.
sentences.linking: Working with poor children, she came‿up with the theory that‿all kids‿have‿an‿innate desire to learn.
tips: 連結: came‿up=ケイマップ, have‿an=ハヴァン, kids‿have
強勢: WORK·ing, THEO·ry, in·NATE, de·SIRE, LEARN

sentences.en: Her method emphasised freedom and responsibility, and was soon adopted worldwide.
sentences.linking: Her method‿emphasised freedom‿and responsibility, and was soon‿adopted worldwide.
tips: 強勢: ME·thod, EM·phasised, FREE·dom, respon·si·BI·lity, a·DOP·ted

sentences.en: A lifelong advocate of peace, her subsequent influence on education endures.
sentences.linking: A lifelong‿advocate‿of peace, her subsequent‿influence‿on‿education‿endures.
tips: 連結: advocate‿of=アドヴォケイトヴ, influence‿on, education‿endures
強勢: LIFE·long, AD·vocate, PEACE, SUB·sequent, edu·CA·tion, en·DURES

passages.themeName: なぜ人はウソをつくのか
passages.jp: ウソは問題に見えるが、実は洗練された脳の証だ。力を使わずに他人を操る技術は進化上の利点であり、巧みな嘘つきはこの特徴を子へ伝えた。人は自分のイメージを誇張するため、または社会的緊張を和らげるために嘘をつく。子どもの発達を調査した科学者は、真実を隠すことを学ぶのは実は節目——脳が成熟に近づいている印——だと突き止めた。

sentences.en: Lying may seem troubling, but it is an indication of a sophisticated brain.
sentences.linking: Lying may seem troubling, but‿it‿is‿an‿indication‿of‿a sophisticated brain.
tips: 連結: it‿is‿an=イティザン, indication‿of, but‿it
強勢: LY·ing, TROU·bling, indi·CA·tion, so·PHIS·ticated, BRAIN
注意音: R(troubling/brain), sophisticated=ソフィスティケイティッド

sentences.en: The skill of manipulating others without force was an evolutionary advantage, and skilled liars passed this trait to their children.
sentences.linking: The skill‿of manipulating‿others without force was‿an‿evolutionary‿advantage, and skilled liars passed this trait to their children.
tips: 連結: skill‿of=スキロヴ, was‿an, manipulating‿others
強勢: ma·NI·pulating, with·OUT, evo·LU·tionary, ad·VAN·tage, TRAIT

sentences.en: We lie to exaggerate our image or to ease social tension.
sentences.linking: We lie to‿exaggerate‿our‿image‿or to‿ease social tension.
tips: 強勢: LIE, ex·AG·gerate, I·mage, EASE, SO·cial, TEN·sion
注意音: R(exaggerate/our/or), exaggerate=イグ·ザ·ジャレイト, image=イミッジ

sentences.en: Scientists who investigated child development found that learning to conceal the truth is actually a milestone — a sign the brain is reaching maturity.
sentences.linking: Scientists who‿investigated child development found that learning to‿conceal the truth‿is‿actually‿a milestone — a sign the brain‿is reaching maturity.
tips: 連結: truth‿is, brain‿is=ブレイニズ, to‿conceal
